name: PH 5 Pendant
description: The iconic PH 5 Pendant delivers glare-free illumination through its layered shade system, originally designed in 1958. Its mathematically calculated curves direct light downward while coloring the light warmly through interior reflectors.

name: Nelson Saucer Bubble Pendant
description: George Nelson's 1952 Saucer Bubble Pendant is a mid-century icon that emits a soft, diffused glow through its translucent polymer skin stretched over a steel wire frame. Its flying saucer silhouette brings sculptural warmth to dining rooms and entryways.

name: Heracleum III LED Chandelier
description: Inspired by the Heracleum plant, this sculptural LED chandelier features dozens of rotatable polycarbonate lenses that scatter light like leaves on delicate metal branches. Each lens can be individually positioned to create a personalized lighting effect.

name: AJ Wall Sconce
description: Arne Jacobsen's AJ Wall Sconce combines geometric precision with functional elegance, featuring an asymmetric shade that directs light downward. Originally designed in 1960 for the SAS Royal Hotel in Copenhagen, it remains a pinnacle of Scandinavian modernism.

name: IC Wall/Ceiling Light
description: The FLOS IC Wall/Ceiling Light balances a blown opaline glass sphere atop a slender brass stem, evoking the tension of a ball poised on a gymnast's fingertip. Its warm, diffused glow makes it equally striking as a wall sconce or ceiling fixture.

name: Twiggy Arc Floor Lamp
description: The Foscarini Twiggy Arc Floor Lamp uses a fiberglass composite stem inspired by a fishing rod, allowing the arc to flex and spring back to form. Adjustable counterweights in the drum shade let you fine-tune the lamp's height and arc reach.

name: Arco LED Floor Lamp
description: A permanent fixture in MoMA's collection, the FLOS Arco LED Floor Lamp features a spun aluminum dome shade arching from a heavy Carrara marble base on a curved stainless steel stem. This LED version of the 1962 icon delivers energy-efficient lighting with the same timeless silhouette.

specs.material: Carrara White marble, Stainless steel, Spun aluminum

name: Grasshopper Floor Lamp
description: Originally designed in 1947 by Greta Grossman, the Grasshopper Floor Lamp rests on three angled tubular steel legs that give it its insect-like stance. The elongated conical shade tilts to direct light precisely where needed, making it a versatile reading or ambient lamp.

name: Tolomeo LED Floor Lamp
description: The Artemide Tolomeo LED Floor Lamp is the floor-standing version of one of the most recognized task lights in modern design. Its fully adjustable articulating arms and rotating head provide precise directional lighting for reading or workspaces.

specs.dimensions: 10" Base Diameter, Max Height 66.5", Max Reach 40.5"

name: Flowerpot VP3 Table Lamp
description: Verner Panton's 1968 Flowerpot VP3 Table Lamp stacks two half-spheres in a playful composition that diffuses light in all directions. Its bold color options and compact form bring a pop of Danish design to bedside tables and desks.

name: Tolomeo Classic Table Lamp
description: The Artemide Tolomeo Classic Table Lamp is a Compasso d'Oro award winner celebrated for its spring-balanced articulating arms and minimalist aluminum construction. Its fully adjustable design makes it the quintessential modern task lamp for home offices and studios.

specs.dimensions: 7" Base Diameter, Max Height 50", Max Reach 31.5"

name: Zephyr LED Smart Ceiling Fan
description: The Zephyr LED Smart Ceiling Fan pairs a silent DC motor with sculptural blades and an artisan-crafted glass LED light kit. Compatible with smart home assistants including Alexa and Google Home, it delivers whisper-quiet airflow with app-based controls.

name: Torque Smart Ceiling Fan
description: The Torque Smart Ceiling Fan descends from a clean beveled canopy on a short downrod, extending three aerodynamically contoured blades for maximum efficiency. Its energy-efficient DC motor is 70% more efficient than traditional AC fans, and it integrates with all major smart home platforms.

name: Lispenard Sofa
description: Architect Neil Logan designed the Lispenard Sofa for Herman Miller with a focus on deep comfort and clean lines. Its plush duck-and-down cushions sit atop a minimal frame with solid hardwood legs available in oak or walnut finishes.

specs.material: Kiln-dried hardwood frame, Duck and down fill

name: Paramount Lounge Chair
description: The Paramount Lounge Chair by Blu Dot is handcrafted in the USA with a kiln-dried hardwood frame that is carefully doweled and corner-blocked for lasting durability. Tufted seat cushions and a removable loose back cushion offer refined comfort with easy maintenance.

name: CH24 Wishbone Chair
description: Hans J. Wegner's 1949 CH24 Wishbone Chair is one of the most celebrated dining chairs in furniture history, featuring a steam-bent solid wood frame and a handwoven paper cord seat that takes a skilled craftsman about one hour to weave. Its signature Y-shaped back support combines structural ingenuity with organic beauty.

specs.dimensions: 21.7" W x 20.1" D x 28.7" H, Seat Height 17.3"
specs.material: Solid oak, Handwoven natural paper cord seat

name: Eames Molded Plywood Dining Chair
description: Charles and Ray Eames revolutionized furniture design with their molded plywood technique, and this dining chair remains a benchmark of mid-century modern seating. The gently curved plywood seat and back are mounted on a slim chrome-plated steel base for a light, elegant profile.

specs.dimensions: 19.4" W x 21.6" D x 29.5" H, Seat Height 18.5"

name: Quill Medium Rug
description: The Nanimarquina Quill Medium Rug features a soft leaf-shaped silhouette with hand-drawn spine details, handtufted from 100% new wool. Designer Nao Tamura crafted each rug to feel like a unique living form, bringing organic texture and warmth to any floor.

name: Shade Rug
description: The Nanimarquina Shade Rug uses gradient color transitions to create a subtle ombre effect that shifts across the surface, handknotted from premium wool. Its minimalist palette adds depth and dimension to living spaces without competing with other design elements.
